_description: Gate definitions for analyze_phase_gaps. Mirrors CustomerProfile.derive_phase() (locked decision D2). Each phase lists the signals required to ADVANCE to the next phase; each signal names the profile field_paths it inspects and a curated FSI discovery question. Keep in sync with agent/customer_profile.py:derive_phase — the parity test enforces this.
_phase_rules_note: Ordered, first-match-wins rules that reproduce CustomerProfile.derive_phase() EXACTLY — including its decision-driven shortcut branches (which can reach proposal/execution on decisions alone, bypassing workload/constraints). `all_of` signals must all hold; `any_of` (when present) requires at least one. The parity test asserts this matches derive_phase across many profiles.

workload_known.description: We know what is being modernized (workload shape captured).
workload_known.question: To size the estate: roughly how many COBOL programs and JCL jobs, and which of CICS / DB2 / IMS / MQ are in play?

constraints_known.description: We know the regulatory / business envelope.
constraints_known.question: What's the envelope — which regulators examine this estate (SOX/FFIEC/OCC/PCI?), any target date, budget band, or downtime tolerance?

has_pattern.description: A migration pattern (6R) has been chosen.
has_pattern.question: Which migration pattern are we anchoring on — rehost, replatform, automated_refactor, refactor, or retire?

has_partner.description: A partner / tool has been selected.
has_partner.question: Which partner or tool are we taking forward (Micro Focus, Blu Age, Heirloom, a GSI, …)? I can rank options if it's still open.

has_target.question: What are the target services / runtime (e.g. Aurora PostgreSQL for DB2, DynamoDB for VSAM, AWS M2 for CICS)? I can compare options per source component.

ge3_active_decisions.description: Three or more active (non-superseded) decisions exist — proxy for an in-flight engagement (matches derive_phase's execution shortcut).
ge3_active_decisions.question: We have several decisions locked — what execution-level detail do you need next (wave sequencing, cutover, runbook)?
